description: Chainlit chat UI + Phoenix trace/annotation integration for EMA Q&A chatbot

tasks.title: Chainlit app — RAG chat with step display and source documents
acceptance_criteria: chainlit>=2.11 added to pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] ui section
app.py at repo root: loads harness/index (mini_corpus.jsonl), runs hybrid retrieval, calls Claude for synthesis, streams answer
cl.Step shows retrieval step: number of docs found, top-3 scores
cl.SourceDocument elements display each retrieved Q&A (question, topic_path, source_url)
chainlit run app.py starts with no MongoDB required
Handles missing benchmark gracefully (no crash if benchmark.jsonl absent)
tasks.notes: Works with mini_corpus.jsonl immediately. No MongoDB needed.

tasks.title: Phoenix trace integration — auto-instrument LlamaIndex + LLM calls
acceptance_criteria: Phoenix launched at startup (px.launch_app or via PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT env var) — documented in README
openinference-instrumentation-llama-index and openinference-instrumentation-anthropic wired in app.py startup
Every retrieval call produces a LlamaIndex span visible in Phoenix UI (localhost:6006)
Every Claude call produces an LLM span with token counts visible in Phoenix UI
Chainlit response includes a 'View trace' link pointing to Phoenix (span URL or project URL)
Phoenix can be disabled by setting PHOENIX_DISABLED=1 env var (graceful fallback)
tasks.notes: arize-phoenix and openinference-instrumentation-llama-index already in pyproject.toml deps. Need to add openinference-instrumentation-anthropic.

tasks.title: Per-step rating → Phoenix span annotations
acceptance_criteria: Each cl.Step has 👍/👎 cl.Action buttons attached
On click: retrieve Phoenix span_id for that step from OpenInference context, call phoenix.Client().spans.add_span_annotation()
Annotation stores: label ('good'/'bad'), score (+1/-1), explanation field (optional free text prompt)
Annotations visible in Phoenix UI under the corresponding trace/span
TASK-027.8 in the main state.json updated to note it is superseded by TASK-UI-003
Unit test: mock Phoenix client, verify annotation payload is correct
tasks.notes: phoenix.Client().spans.add_span_annotation() confirmed available in v15. This feeds TASK-029 trajectory labeling.

tasks.title: Agent integration — ReAct agent steps in Chainlit (after TASK-027)
acceptance_criteria: app.py mode flag: --mode rag (default, works now) | --mode agent (requires TASK-027)
In agent mode: each tool call (retrieve, follow_cross_refs, filter_by_concept) appears as a cl.Step
Each agent step has 👍/👎 rating buttons → Phoenix annotations
Streaming works for agent mode (intermediate steps stream as they complete)
Final answer shown with all supporting steps collapsible
tasks.notes: Deferred until TASK-027 (ReActAgent) is complete. TASK-UI-001–003 work independently.
